isolation.qemuConfig: virt,gic-version=2, cortex-a53, 4 SMP cores, 256M RAM, virtio-gpu + keyboard + 9p

steps.action: Boot QEMU and verify display pipeline running
steps.expected: Title bar with icon, title, clock; cursor in content area
steps.observed: Title bar shows document icon, 'Text' title, clock '13:57:32'; cursor visible at top-left of content area

steps.expected: Character appears in content area, cursor advances
steps.observed: Character 'h' appeared in content area, cursor positioned after it
No errors found in serial log

assertions.title: Clock tick renders correctly with incremental path

steps.expected: Clock time visible in title bar

steps.observed: Both screenshots show 'h' in content area — identical, no artifacts
Text 'helloworld' rendered in monospace font, evenly spaced

steps.expected: Cursor visible at end of typed text
steps.observed: Cursor visible after 'd' at correct position

steps.action: Verify title bar with icon and title
steps.observed: Document icon (page shape) and 'Text' title visible in title bar
Gradient shadow visible below title bar separating chrome from content
Dark background with light text — consistent with OS design

assertions.title: Rapid typing produces no visual artifacts

steps.action: Send Enter key to create new line
Send 26 keystrokes (a-z) rapidly via QEMU monitor with no delay
steps.expected: All 26 characters appear with no missing chars or artifacts
steps.observed: All 26 characters 'abcdefghijklmnopqrstuvwxyz' rendered correctly on second line

steps.action: Verify no stale pixels or corruption
steps.observed: Clean monospace rendering, no stale pixels, no corruption. Cursor at correct position after 'z'
No errors found in serial log

summary: Tested 4 assertions: 4 passed, 0 failed. All QEMU visual assertions verified successfully. Keystroke rendering (VAL-CROSS-001), clock incremental updates (VAL-CROSS-002), full visual rendering quality (VAL-CROSS-005), and rapid typing stress (VAL-CROSS-006) all produce correct visual output with no artifacts and no serial errors.
